assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response correctly explains that Regular channel clusters will be force-upgraded to the next available version, with specific timeline of 1-2 weeks after EoS

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'additional time (typically 6+ months)' but doesn't specify the 24-month support window or clearly explain that 1.30 EoS is significantly later for Extended channel

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response mentions force upgrade for 'no channel' clusters but doesn't specifically explain the distinction between control plane and node pool upgrades, or that node pools are upgraded even with 'no auto-upgrade' configured

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to temporarily defer EoS force-upgrades for 30 days

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends proactive upgrade as the preferred option and provides specific commands and action plans

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Cloud Logging notifications as a tool for EoS planning

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the deprecation insights dashboard, though it does mention reviewing breaking changes and deprecated APIs generally

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response does not use the term 'snowflaking' or explicitly warn against manually freezing versions, though it does recommend against static versioning indirectly
